name: Staple Product, No Inducement
description: Generic commodity product with no supplier misconduct. Expected: Unlikely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Improved fastening mechanism for modular panels
patent_context.claim_description: A method of fastening modular panels using a specific clip arrangement

product.product_description: Generic metal clips sold at hardware stores for general fastening purposes
product.staple_reasoning: Metal clips are a widely available commodity product with many non-infringing uses

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier sells general hardware products and has no specific knowledge of the patent

evidence_flags.evidence_notes: No evidence of any supplier conduct directed toward patent infringement

name: Non-Staple Product, Supplier Knowledge
description: Purpose-built product from a former licensee with full knowledge and active marketing. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Specialised dosing apparatus for agricultural chemicals
patent_context.claim_description: A dosing apparatus with a specific valve and metering arrangement for controlled release of agricultural chemicals

product.product_description: A specialised metering device designed specifically for use with the patented dosing apparatus. Has no substantial non-infringing use.
product.staple_reasoning: The product is specifically designed for a single application that falls within the patent claims

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier is a former licensee whose licence was terminated. Supplier has detailed knowledge of the patent and its claims.
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Product manual includes installation instructions that replicate the patented method
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Marketing materials reference compatibility with the patented system by name

evidence_flags.evidence_notes: Former licensee with full knowledge; marketing materials and product manuals available as evidence

name: Staple Product + Strong Inducement
description: Commodity product, but supplier was warned and actively promotes infringing use. Expected: Possible Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Method for enhanced concrete curing using polymer additives
patent_context.claim_description: A method of curing concrete involving the application of a specific polymer additive at controlled temperatures

product.product_description: A polymer additive commonly used in construction for various concrete treatment purposes. Widely available from multiple suppliers.
product.staple_reasoning: Polymer additives for concrete are widely sold commodity products with many uses beyond the patented method

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier was sent a cease-and-desist letter identifying the patent and explaining the infringing use. Supplier continued sales.
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Supplier's product data sheet includes a specific section titled 'Enhanced Curing Method' that replicates the patented process step by step
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Supplier ran trade magazine advertisements promoting their product specifically for the enhanced curing method described in the patent

evidence_flags.evidence_notes: Cease-and-desist letter on file; advertising materials and product data sheet available

name: Mixed / Incomplete Case
description: Incomplete data with uncertain supplier conduct. Tests graceful degradation. Expected: Insufficient Information.

patent_context.patent_title: Adaptive cooling system for industrial machinery
patent_context.claim_description: A cooling system comprising a variable-speed pump and adaptive thermostat for maintaining optimal operating temperature

product.product_description: A variable-speed pump module marketed for industrial cooling applications

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier may have seen the patent but this is not confirmed

name: Direct Infringement — All Elements Matched
description: Smart irrigation controller where all claim elements are present in the accused product. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Smart irrigation controller with soil moisture feedback
patent_context.claim_description: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor, a wireless communication module, and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm

claims.claim_text: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor; a wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data; and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm that adjusts irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings
claim_elements: wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data
adaptive watering schedule algorithm adjusting irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings
claims.accused_product_description: The AquaSmart Pro controller includes a built-in soil moisture sensor, WiFi module that transmits moisture data to a cloud service, and a proprietary algorithm that dynamically adjusts watering schedules in real time based on soil moisture sensor readings.

element_mapping_facts.elem_1: Accused product contains a soil moisture sensor — matched
element_mapping_facts.elem_2: WiFi module transmits sensor data to cloud — satisfies wireless communication module
element_mapping_facts.elem_3: Proprietary algorithm adjusts irrigation timing based on real-time moisture readings — present

name: Direct Infringement — Missing Elements
description: Thermal insulation panel where key elements (phase-change core, aerogel) are absent. Expected: Unlikely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Multi-layer thermal insulation panel with phase-change core
patent_context.claim_description: A thermal insulation panel comprising an outer reflective layer, a phase-change material core, an inner aerogel insulation layer, and a thermally conductive adhesive

claim_elements: phase-change material core for thermal energy storage
thermally conductive adhesive bonding the layers
claims.accused_product_description: The ThermoGuard Standard Panel uses a reflective outer foil and a fibreglass insulation core. It does not contain any phase-change material or aerogel layer. Layers are bonded with standard construction adhesive.

element_mapping_facts.elem_2: No phase-change material in the product — absent
element_mapping_facts.elem_3: Uses fibreglass, not aerogel — does not satisfy this element
element_mapping_facts.elem_4: Standard adhesive, not thermally conductive — not present

name: Both Direct + s117 Infringement
description: Hydroponic nutrient system — all claim elements matched AND supplier has knowledge/inducement. Both skills triggered. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Automated nutrient dispensing system for hydroponic cultivation
patent_context.claim_description: An automated nutrient dispensing system comprising a pH sensor, an EC sensor, a programmable dispensing controller, and a nutrient reservoir with calibrated dosing pumps

product.product_description: A specialised nutrient dispensing module designed for integration with hydroponic systems
product.staple_reasoning: The module is purpose-built for hydroponic nutrient dispensing with no substantial non-infringing use

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier is a former distribution partner briefed on the patent during the partnership
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Product manual describes integration with the patented system by name
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Marketing materials promote the module as a drop-in replacement for the patented system

claim_elements: nutrient reservoir with calibrated dosing pumps
claims.accused_product_description: The HydroNutrient Pro module includes integrated pH and EC sensors, a microcontroller-based programmable dispensing unit, and connects to a nutrient reservoir via calibrated peristaltic dosing pumps.

element_mapping_facts.elem_1: pH sensor present and integrated — matched
element_mapping_facts.elem_2: EC sensor present and integrated — matched
element_mapping_facts.elem_3: Microcontroller-based programmable dispensing unit — satisfies this element
element_mapping_facts.elem_4: Calibrated peristaltic dosing pumps connected to reservoir — present

evidence_flags.evidence_notes: Former partner with detailed knowledge; marketing materials and product manual available

name: Claim Construction Recommended
description: Noise-cancelling headphone with ambiguous claim terms. Disputed elements cause uncertainty. Expected: Possible Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Noise-cancelling headphone with adaptive frequency filtering
patent_context.claim_description: A noise-cancelling headphone comprising an adaptive frequency filter that dynamically adjusts noise cancellation parameters based on ambient sound profile analysis

claim_elements: based on ambient sound profile analysis
claims.accused_product_description: The SilentPro X1 headphone uses active noise cancellation with a multi-band equaliser that responds to environmental noise levels. The manufacturer describes it as having intelligent sound adaptation but the internal mechanism is proprietary.
claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'adaptive frequency filter' is disputed — unclear whether a multi-band equaliser qualifies. The phrase 'ambient sound profile analysis' is ambiguous — does simple dB-level measurement count, or must there be frequency-domain decomposition?

name: CC: Clear Wording, No Sensitivity
description: LED strip light with clear claim terms. Claim construction flagged but no ambiguity detected. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Waterproof LED strip light with adhesive backing
patent_context.claim_description: A waterproof LED strip light comprising a flexible PCB with surface-mounted LEDs, a silicone encapsulation layer, and a pressure-sensitive adhesive backing

claims.claim_text: A waterproof LED strip light comprising a flexible PCB with surface-mounted LEDs, a silicone encapsulation layer, and a pressure-sensitive adhesive backing
claims.accused_product_description: The LumiStrip Pro uses a flexible printed circuit board with SMD LEDs, a silicone waterproof coating, and 3M adhesive tape on the back for mounting.

element_mapping_facts.elem_1: Flexible PCB with SMD LEDs present — matched
element_mapping_facts.elem_3: 3M adhesive tape on back — satisfies pressure-sensitive adhesive backing

name: CC: Disputed Terms Affect Matching
description: Smart irrigation controller with disputed 'wireless module' and 'adaptive algorithm'. Broad view: likely; Narrow view: unlikely. Expected: Possible Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Smart garden irrigation system with adaptive scheduling
patent_context.claim_description: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor, a wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data, and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings

claims.claim_text: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor, a wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data, and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings

structured_elements.text: wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data
adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing
based on real-time soil moisture readings

claims.accused_product_description: The GreenBot G5 controller includes a capacitive soil sensor, Bluetooth data link to a phone app, and a preset watering timer that users can manually adjust after checking soil readings on their phone.

element_mapping_facts.e2: Bluetooth data link may or may not constitute a 'wireless communication module' — the claim may require a dedicated module rather than a phone link

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'wireless communication module' is disputed — does a Bluetooth phone link qualify as a 'module'? The phrase 'adaptive watering schedule algorithm' is also contested — the accused product uses manual timer adjustment, not an autonomous algorithm. The term 'configured to' may imply either inherent capability or active configuration.
claims.specification_notes: The patent specification describes a dedicated wireless radio module and an on-device algorithm that operates without user intervention. This suggests a narrower interpretation of both the communication module and the adaptive algorithm.

name: CC: Broad vs Narrow Changes Result
description: Robotic vacuum with disputed 'close proximity' sensing and 'adapted to' language. Broad view: likely infringement. Narrow view: unlikely. Expected: Possible Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Robotic vacuum cleaner with obstacle avoidance mechanism
patent_context.claim_description: A robotic vacuum cleaner comprising a navigation module, an obstacle detection mechanism arranged to sense objects in close proximity, and a path-planning unit adapted to dynamically adjust cleaning routes

claims.claim_text: A robotic vacuum cleaner comprising a navigation module, an obstacle detection mechanism arranged to sense objects in close proximity, and a path-planning unit adapted to dynamically adjust cleaning routes

structured_elements.text: obstacle detection mechanism arranged to sense objects in close proximity
path-planning unit adapted to dynamically adjust cleaning routes

claims.accused_product_description: The CleanBot 3000 is a robotic vacuum with LiDAR-based room mapping, infrared bump sensors that detect collisions, and firmware that replans the cleaning path when the robot hits an obstacle.

element_mapping_facts.e1: CleanBot 3000 is a robotic vacuum cleaner — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: LiDAR-based room mapping — satisfies navigation module
element_mapping_facts.e3: Infrared bump sensors detect collisions but may not sense objects 'in close proximity' before contact — ambiguous
element_mapping_facts.e4: Firmware replans path after collision, but unclear if this counts as 'adapted to dynamically adjust' — the patent may require anticipatory adjustment, not reactive

claims.disputed_element_notes: The phrase 'in close proximity' is disputed — does it require non-contact sensing, or do bump sensors count? The term 'adapted to dynamically adjust' is ambiguous — does reactive replanning after collision satisfy this, or must the system anticipate obstacles? The term 'mechanism' could cover any sensor arrangement or only the specific types described in the specification.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes ultrasonic proximity sensors that detect obstacles before contact and a real-time path adjustment system. This supports a narrower reading requiring non-contact detection and anticipatory path adjustment.

name: CC + DI + s117: All Three Skills
description: Air quality monitor — disputed 'wireless module' + non-staple product from former contractor. All three skills execute. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Portable air quality monitoring device with wireless alert system
patent_context.claim_description: A portable air quality monitoring device comprising a particulate matter sensor module, a gas detection unit, a wireless communication module configured to transmit alert data, and a threshold-based notification mechanism

product.product_description: A compact air quality sensor kit sold to building managers and HVAC contractors for indoor air monitoring.
product.staple_reasoning: The AirSafe Mini is specifically designed for the patented air quality monitoring and alert application

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Former contract manufacturer with detailed knowledge of the patented system design.
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Setup guide references alert thresholds from the patent specification.
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Marketing materials describe the product as a 'compatible alternative' to the patented monitoring system.

claims.claim_text: A portable air quality monitoring device comprising a particulate matter sensor module, a gas detection unit, a wireless communication module configured to transmit alert data, and a threshold-based notification mechanism

structured_elements.text: wireless communication module configured to transmit alert data

claims.accused_product_description: The AirSafe Mini includes a PM2.5 sensor, a VOC gas sensor, WiFi connectivity for sending data to a cloud dashboard, and an app-based push notification system with configurable thresholds.

element_mapping_facts.e1: AirSafe Mini is a portable air quality monitoring device — matched
element_mapping_facts.e4: WiFi connectivity transmits data to cloud — may or may not constitute a 'wireless communication module' as a distinct component
element_mapping_facts.e5: App-based push notifications with configurable thresholds — matches threshold-based notification

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'wireless communication module' is disputed — is WiFi to a cloud dashboard the same as a dedicated 'module configured to transmit alert data'? The term 'module' may require a discrete hardware component.
claims.specification_notes: The patent specification describes a dedicated radio transceiver module for direct device-to-device alert transmission, not cloud-mediated notifications.

evidence.evidence_notes: Former contract manufacturer with detailed knowledge; marketing materials and setup guide available.

name: S3.5: Stable Under Both Modes
description: Greenhouse vent system — disputed 'control module' but both broad and narrow readings lead to infringement. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Automated greenhouse ventilation system with temperature feedback
patent_context.claim_description: An automated greenhouse ventilation system comprising a temperature sensor array, a motorised vent actuator, and a control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

claims.claim_text: An automated greenhouse ventilation system comprising a temperature sensor array, a motorised vent actuator, and a control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

structured_elements.text: control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

claims.accused_product_description: The GreenAir Pro is an automated greenhouse vent controller with three digital temperature sensors, electric linear actuators on the roof vents, and a programmable logic controller that opens vents when temperature exceeds a user-set threshold.

element_mapping_facts.e1: GreenAir Pro is an automated greenhouse ventilation system — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Three digital temperature sensors constitute a temperature sensor array — matched
element_mapping_facts.e3: Electric linear actuators on roof vents — satisfies motorised vent actuator — matched
element_mapping_facts.e4: PLC opens vents when temp exceeds threshold — satisfies control module, though term 'module' is disputed

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'control module' in element e4 is disputed — does a general-purpose PLC configured via software qualify as a 'module', or must it be a dedicated hardware controller? The specification describes both PLCs and dedicated microcontrollers as suitable.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes both dedicated microcontrollers and programmable controllers as suitable implementations of the control module.

name: S3.5: Broad/Narrow Diverge
description: Fitness tracker — 'biometric module' requires biometrics (narrow) or any auth (broad). Broad: likely infringement. Narrow: unlikely. Expected: Possible Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Wearable fitness tracker with biometric authentication module
patent_context.claim_description: A wearable fitness tracker comprising a heart rate sensor, a biometric authentication module adapted to verify user identity, and a wireless data transmission unit configured to transmit health data to a paired device

claims.claim_text: A wearable fitness tracker comprising a heart rate sensor, a biometric authentication module adapted to verify user identity, and a wireless data transmission unit configured to transmit health data to a paired device

structured_elements.text: biometric authentication module adapted to verify user identity
wireless data transmission unit configured to transmit health data to a paired device

claims.accused_product_description: The FitBand X200 is a wearable fitness band with an optical heart rate sensor, a PIN-based lock screen, Bluetooth connectivity that syncs daily step and heart rate summaries to a phone app.

element_mapping_facts.e1: FitBand X200 is a wearable fitness tracker — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Optical heart rate sensor present — matched
element_mapping_facts.e3: PIN-based lock screen — unclear if this is a 'biometric authentication module' — no biometric sensing present
element_mapping_facts.e4: Bluetooth syncs data to phone — may or may not constitute a 'wireless data transmission unit' as a distinct component

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'biometric authentication module' is disputed — the accused product uses PIN-based authentication, not biometrics. Under a broad reading, any user verification mechanism might suffice; under a narrow reading, biometric sensing hardware is required. The term 'wireless data transmission unit' is also contested — is Bluetooth data sync via a phone app the same as a dedicated transmission unit?
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes fingerprint and heart-rate-pattern biometric authentication and a dedicated Bluetooth Low Energy transceiver module for continuous health data streaming.

name: S3.5: Divergent + Insufficient
description: Solar panel mount — too many missing/uncertain elements under any reading. Expected: Possible Infringement (divergent but insufficient in one mode).

patent_context.patent_title: Modular solar panel mounting system with adaptive tilt mechanism
patent_context.claim_description: A modular solar panel mounting system comprising a base frame assembly, an adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle, a wind load sensor unit, and a control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

claims.claim_text: A modular solar panel mounting system comprising a base frame assembly, an adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle, a wind load sensor unit, and a control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

structured_elements.text: adaptive tilt mechanism arranged to adjust panel angle
control apparatus configured to optimise energy capture

claims.accused_product_description: The SunTrack Lite is a solar panel mounting rail with a fixed tilt bracket. It has no motorised components, no wind sensors, and no electronic control system. Tilt angle is set manually during installation.

element_mapping_facts.e1: SunTrack Lite is a solar panel mounting system — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Mounting rail with bracket — may or may not qualify as a 'base frame assembly'
element_mapping_facts.e3: Fixed tilt bracket, manual adjustment only — unclear if 'adaptive tilt mechanism' requires motorised or automatic adjustment
element_mapping_facts.e5: No electronic control system — unclear if manual installation angle selection could ever be a 'control apparatus configured to optimise'

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'adaptive tilt mechanism' is disputed — does it require automated or motorised adjustment, or could a manually adjustable bracket suffice? The term 'control apparatus configured to optimise' is also highly ambiguous — the accused product has no electronics. The 'base frame assembly' term could be read broadly to include any mounting rail or narrowly as a specific structural assembly.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes a motorised actuator for the tilt mechanism and a microcontroller-based control apparatus with software optimisation algorithms.

name: S3.5: DI Diverges + s117 Strong
description: HVAC controller — DI diverges (broad likely, narrow unlikely) but s117 is strong due to non-staple + former JV partner. Expected: Likely Infringement.

patent_context.patent_title: Smart HVAC control unit with predictive maintenance module
patent_context.claim_description: A smart HVAC control unit comprising a temperature and humidity sensor array, a compressor control module configured to modulate cooling output, a predictive maintenance module adapted to forecast component failure, and a wireless reporting unit

product.product_description: A smart HVAC controller sold to commercial building operators for climate management and remote monitoring.
product.staple_reasoning: The ClimaCore Pro is specifically designed for the patented HVAC control and predictive maintenance application with no substantial non-infringing use

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier was a former joint venture partner with the patent holder and participated in the development of the patented system.
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Installation manual references the patented predictive maintenance algorithm by name and includes configuration steps from the patent specification.
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Trade show materials describe the product as 'fully compatible with and equivalent to' the patented system.

claims.claim_text: A smart HVAC control unit comprising a temperature and humidity sensor array, a compressor control module configured to modulate cooling output, a predictive maintenance module adapted to forecast component failure, and a wireless reporting unit

structured_elements.text: compressor control module configured to modulate cooling output
predictive maintenance module adapted to forecast component failure

claims.accused_product_description: The ClimaCore Pro includes temperature and humidity sensors, a variable-speed compressor driver board, a basic fault logging system that records error codes but does not predict failures, and WiFi connectivity for sending status reports to a cloud dashboard.

element_mapping_facts.e1: ClimaCore Pro is a smart HVAC control unit — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Temperature and humidity sensors present — matched
element_mapping_facts.e3: Variable-speed compressor driver board — may or may not be a 'compressor control module' with 'modulate cooling output' capability
element_mapping_facts.e4: Fault logging system records errors but does not predict failures — unclear if this is a 'predictive maintenance module'
element_mapping_facts.e5: WiFi to cloud dashboard — may or may not qualify as a 'wireless reporting unit' distinct from general connectivity

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'compressor control module configured to modulate' is disputed — the accused product uses a driver board, not a dedicated module. The term 'predictive maintenance module adapted to forecast' is highly contested — the accused product only logs faults, it does not predict failures. The 'wireless reporting unit' may require a dedicated reporting component vs general WiFi.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes a dedicated compressor control ASIC and a machine-learning-based predictive maintenance system. The wireless reporting unit is described as a separate radio transceiver with its own processor.

evidence.evidence_notes: Former JV partner; trade show materials, installation manuals, and customer testimony about compatibility claims all available.
